Mature T-Cell Lymphomas — The Subway System

🎯 Look at the lit symbol — say out loud what it is and what it represents (and the trap). Then reveal.

What you see
What it encodes
The board trap
Use ← → arrow keys · click a dot to jump · ▶ plays & narrates the scene
⚠️ Board traps 11
  1. PTCL-NOS (Station 1) — Don't call it ALCL — PTCL-NOS lacks CD30 strong/uniform positivity and the hallmark cells; it is the leftover bucket.
  2. ALCL ALK+ (Station 2) — ALK-positivity = good prognosis in ALCL. Don't confuse with ALK in lung cancer. ALK-negative ALCL (40-50%) is worse and DUSP22 rearrangement subset behaves like ALK+.
  3. ALCL ALK- (Station 2) — Primary cutaneous ALCL is a separate indolent skin-limited entity — don't treat it like systemic ALK- ALCL with aggressive chemo.
  4. AITL (Station 3) — The polyclonal hypergammaglobulinemia and autoimmune features can mimic infection or autoimmune disease — biopsy shows arborizing high endothelial venules and TFH markers.
  5. ATLL (Station 4) — Hypercalcemia + lytic lesions can mimic myeloma — but ATLL has circulating flower cells and HTLV-1 serology. Strongyloides hyperinfection risk.
  6. NK/T-Cell Nasal (Station 5) — Don't mistake nasal destruction for granulomatosis with polyangiitis or invasive fungal sinusitis — EBER in-situ hybridization is positive in NK/T lymphoma.
  7. Mycosis Fungoides (Station 6) — Early patch-stage MF mimics eczema/psoriasis for years — repeat biopsies often needed. Loss of CD7 supports clonal T-cell process.
  8. Sezary Syndrome (Station 6) — Erythroderma has many causes (drug, psoriasis, atopic) — Sezary requires demonstrating the clonal blood population, not just red skin.
  9. EATL — In a celiac patient with new abdominal symptoms or refractoriness, think EATL — not just a celiac flare.
  10. CD30 / Brentuximab Target — Brentuximab works on CD30 expression level — not all PTCL-NOS express enough CD30 to benefit, unlike uniformly CD30+ ALCL.
  11. T-Cell Origin (Tunnel) — These are MATURE (peripheral) T-cell lymphomas — distinct from T-lymphoblastic lymphoma/T-ALL, which is a precursor (TdT+) neoplasm of thymic origin.
📖 Read-through — all 11 symbols
1. PTCL-NOS (Station 1)
Peripheral T-cell lymphoma, not otherwise specified is the most common mature T-cell lymphoma in the West (~30%). Diagnosis of exclusion after ruling out specific entities. Aggressive with poor prognosis; CHOP/CHOEP first-line, 5-yr OS only ~30%.
⚠️ Don't call it ALCL — PTCL-NOS lacks CD30 strong/uniform positivity and the hallmark cells; it is the leftover bucket.
2. ALCL ALK+ (Station 2)
Anaplastic large cell lymphoma, ALK-positive carries t(2;5) NPM-ALK translocation. CD30+ always. Occurs in young patients; FAVORABLE prognosis, 5-yr OS 70-80%. Hallmark cells with horseshoe nuclei.
⚠️ ALK-positivity = good prognosis in ALCL. Don't confuse with ALK in lung cancer. ALK-negative ALCL (40-50%) is worse and DUSP22 rearrangement subset behaves like ALK+.
3. ALCL ALK- (Station 2)
ALK-negative ALCL is CD30+ but lacks ALK translocation; older patients, intermediate prognosis (5-yr OS 40-50%). Systemic (not primary cutaneous) form. Brentuximab vedotin (anti-CD30) highly active.
⚠️ Primary cutaneous ALCL is a separate indolent skin-limited entity — don't treat it like systemic ALK- ALCL with aggressive chemo.
4. AITL (Station 3)
Angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma arises from T-follicular-helper (TFH) cells. Classic triad: generalized lymphadenopathy, hypergammaglobulinemia, autoimmune phenomena (Coombs+ hemolysis, rash). EBV-driven B-cell expansion in background. CD10+, BCL6+, PD1+, CXCL13+.
⚠️ The polyclonal hypergammaglobulinemia and autoimmune features can mimic infection or autoimmune disease — biopsy shows arborizing high endothelial venules and TFH markers.
5. ATLL (Station 4)
Adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma is caused by HTLV-1 (endemic Japan, Caribbean). Four subtypes: acute, lymphomatous, chronic, smoldering. Acute/lymphomatous are aggressive. Hypercalcemia with lytic bone lesions, flower cells, CD4+CD25+.
⚠️ Hypercalcemia + lytic lesions can mimic myeloma — but ATLL has circulating flower cells and HTLV-1 serology. Strongyloides hyperinfection risk.
6. NK/T-Cell Nasal (Station 5)
Extranodal NK/T-cell lymphoma, nasal type is EBV-driven, angiocentric/angiodestructive causing midfacial destruction (lethal midline granuloma). CD56+, cytoplasmic CD3+. Treated with L-asparaginase-based SMILE regimen plus radiation.
⚠️ Don't mistake nasal destruction for granulomatosis with polyangiitis or invasive fungal sinusitis — EBER in-situ hybridization is positive in NK/T lymphoma.
7. Mycosis Fungoides (Station 6)
Mycosis fungoides is the most common cutaneous T-cell lymphoma — indolent, skin-limited patches/plaques/tumors. CD4+ T-cells with cerebriform (Sezary/Lutzner) nuclei, epidermotropism with Pautrier microabscesses. Skin-directed therapy early.
⚠️ Early patch-stage MF mimics eczema/psoriasis for years — repeat biopsies often needed. Loss of CD7 supports clonal T-cell process.
8. Sezary Syndrome (Station 6)
Sezary syndrome is the leukemic/erythrodermic variant of CTCL: triad of erythroderma, lymphadenopathy, and ≥1000/µL circulating Sezary cells. Worse prognosis than MF. CD4+CD7- clone in blood; treat systemically.
⚠️ Erythroderma has many causes (drug, psoriasis, atopic) — Sezary requires demonstrating the clonal blood population, not just red skin.
9. EATL
Enteropathy-associated T-cell lymphoma is a gut T-cell lymphoma arising in refractory celiac disease (Type I). Presents with abdominal pain, perforation, obstruction. Poor prognosis. Monomorphic epitheliotropic intestinal TCL is the Type II (non-celiac) variant.
⚠️ In a celiac patient with new abdominal symptoms or refractoriness, think EATL — not just a celiac flare.
10. CD30 / Brentuximab Target
CD30 is the unifying target across ALCL (and CD30+ PTCL, MF). Brentuximab vedotin is an anti-CD30 antibody-drug conjugate (MMAE payload). CHOP+brentuximab (ECHELON-2) improved survival in CD30+ PTCL.
⚠️ Brentuximab works on CD30 expression level — not all PTCL-NOS express enough CD30 to benefit, unlike uniformly CD30+ ALCL.
11. T-Cell Origin (Tunnel)
The whole 'underground' represents mature/peripheral T-cell and NK-cell neoplasms — post-thymic. Collectively ~10-15% of NHL, more aggressive than B-cell counterparts overall except indolent cutaneous forms.
⚠️ These are MATURE (peripheral) T-cell lymphomas — distinct from T-lymphoblastic lymphoma/T-ALL, which is a precursor (TdT+) neoplasm of thymic origin.
